title: Client for Central Bank APIs
text: bbk minimal R client following APIs: Banco de España (BdE) Banco de Portugal (BdP) Bank International Settlements (BIS) Bank Canada (BoC) Bank England (BoE) Bank Japan (BoJ) Banque de France (BdF) Deutsche Bundesbank (BBk) European Central Bank (ECB) Norges Bank (NoB) Sveriges Riksbank (SRb) Swiss National Bank (SNB) Österreichische Nationalbank (OeNB) future, may extended central banks financial institutions. Feel free open issue specific request.

title: Client for Central Bank APIs
text: can install released version bbk CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("bbk") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("m-muecke/bbk")

title: Client for Central Bank APIs
text: bbk functions prefixed according central bank access (bbk_, ecb_, snb_, bdp_, bis_, boe_, boj_, bde_, bdf_, onb_, boc_, nob_, srb_) follow naming conventions respective APIs. typical workflow involves: Finding time series identifier relevant central bank’s website Using appropriate bbk function retrieve data
code: library(bbk)  # Deutsche Bundesbank - fetch 10 year daily yield curve yield_curve <- bbk_data(   flow = "BBSIS",   key = "D.I.ZAR.ZI.EUR.S1311.B.A604.R10XX.R.A.A._Z._Z.A",   start_period = "2020-01-01" )

title: Client for Central Bank APIs
text: BIS: R package downloading data Bank International Settlements (BIS). BISdata: R package downloading data Bank International Settlements (BIS). boe: R package downloading data Bank England (BoE). BOJ: R package downloading data Bank Japan (BoJ). SNBdata: R package downloading data Swiss National Bank (SNB). bundesbank: R scripts downloading time-series data Bundesbank. ecb: R interface European Central Bank’s Statistical Data Warehouse (SDW) API. oenb: R package accessing OeNB’s API. pdfetch: R package downloading economic financial time series public sources. readsdmx: R package reading SDMX data metadata. rsdmx: R package reading SDMX data metadata. rwebstat: R package accessing Banque de France’s Webstat API. tidyBdE: R package accessing Banco de España’s API. valet: R client Bank Canada Valet API.

what: bbk: Client for Central Bank APIs — bbk-package
title: bbk: Client for Central Bank APIs — bbk-package
text: client retrieving data metadata major central bank APIs. supports access 'Bundesbank SDMX Web Service API' (https://www.bundesbank.de/en/statistics/time-series-databases/help--sdmx-web-service/web-service-interface-data), 'Swiss National Bank Data Portal' (https://data.snb.ch/en), 'European Central Bank Data Portal API' (https://data.ecb.europa.eu/help/api/overview), 'Bank England Interactive Statistical Database' (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/boeapps/database), 'Banco de España API' (https://www.bde.es/webbe/en/estadisticas/recursos/api-estadisticas-bde.html), 'Bank International Settlements SDMX Web Service' (https://stats.bis.org/api-doc/v1/), 'Banque de France Web Service' (https://webstat.banque-france.fr/en/pages/guide-migration-api/), 'Norges Bank SDMX Web Service' (https://www.norges-bank./en/topics/Statistics/open-data/), 'Oesterreichische Nationalbank Web Service' (https://www.oenb./en/Statistics/User-Defined-Tables/webservice.html), 'Bank Canada Valet API' (https://www.bankofcanada.ca/valet/docs), 'Banco de Portugal BPstat API' (https://bpstat.bportugal.pt/data/docs), 'Bank Japan Statistics API' (https://www.stat-search.boj..jp/ssi/html/nme_a_en.html).

title: Fetch Deutsche Bundesbank (BBk) data — bbk_data
text: flow (character(1)) flow query, 5-8 characters. See bbk_metadata() available dataflows. key (NULL | character()) series keys query. start_period (NULL | character(1) | integer(1)) start date data. Supported formats: YYYY annual data (e.g., `2019“) YYYY-S[1-2] semi-annual data (e.g., "2019-S1") YYYY-Q[1-4] quarterly data (e.g., "2019-Q1") YYYY-MM monthly data (e.g., "2019-01") YYYY-W[01-53] weekly data (e.g., "2019-W01") YYYY-MM-DD daily business data (e.g., "2019-01-01") NULL, start date restriction applied (data retrieved earliest available date). Default NULL. end_period (NULL | character(1) | integer(1)) end date data, format start_period. NULL, end date restriction applied (data retrieved recent available date). Default NULL. first_n (NULL | numeric(1))  Number observations retrieve start series. NULL, restriction applied. Default NULL. last_n (NULL | numeric(1)) Number observations retrieve end series. NULL, restriction applied. Default NULL.

text: flow (character(1)) dataflow query. See bis_metadata() available dataflows. key (NULL | character()) series keys query using dot-separated dimension values (e.g., "M.CH"). Use + multiple values one dimension (e.g., "M.CH+US"). NULL, data flow returned. Default NULL. start_period (NULL | character(1) | integer(1)) Start date data. Supported formats: YYYY annual data (e.g., 2019) YYYY-S[1-2] semi-annual data (e.g., "2019-S1") YYYY-Q[1-4] quarterly data (e.g., "2019-Q1") YYYY-MM monthly data (e.g., "2019-01") YYYY-MM-DD daily data (e.g., "2019-01-01") NULL, start date restriction applied (data retrieved earliest available date). Default NULL. end_period (NULL | character(1) | integer(1)) End date data, format start_period. NULL, end date restriction applied (data retrieved recent available date). Default NULL. first_n (NULL | numeric(1)) Number observations retrieve start series. NULL, restriction applied. Default NULL. last_n (NULL | numeric(1)) Number observations retrieve end series. NULL, restriction applied. Default NULL.

title: Fetch Bank of Japan (BoJ) data — boj_data
text: db (character(1)) database code query (e.g., "FM08" foreign exchange rates). See API manual available databases. code (character()) One series codes query (e.g., "FXERD01" USD/JPY spot rate). Maximum 250 codes per request. codes must frequency. Use boj_metadata() find available codes. start_date (NULL | character(1) | integer(1)) Start date data. Format depends frequency: "YYYYMMDD" YYYY daily, "YYYYMM" monthly, "YYYYQQ" quarterly (QQ 01-04), "YYYY" annual. NULL, available data returned. Default NULL. end_date (NULL | character(1) | integer(1)) End date data, format start_date. NULL, data latest available date returned. Default NULL. lang (character(1)) Language series names, either "en" "jp". Default "en".

title: Fetch European Central Bank (ECB) data — ecb_data
text: https://data.ecb.europa.eu/help/api/data

title: Fetch European Central Bank (ECB) data — ecb_data
text: flow (character(1)) Flow query. key (NULL | character()) series keys query. start_period (NULL | character(1) | integer(1)) Start date data. Supported formats: YYYY annual data (e.g., 2019) YYYY-S[1-2] semi-annual data (e.g., "2019-S1") YYYY-Q[1-4] quarterly data (e.g., "2019-Q1") YYYY-MM monthly data (e.g., "2019-01") YYYY-W[01-53] weekly data (e.g., "2019-W01") YYYY-MM-DD daily business data (e.g., "2019-01-01") NULL, start date restriction applied (data retrieved earliest available date). Default NULL. end_period (NULL | character(1) | integer(1)) End date data, format start_period. NULL, end date restriction applied (data retrieved recent available date). Default NULL. first_n (NULL | numeric(1)) Number observations retrieve start series. NULL, restriction applied. Default NULL. last_n (NULL | numeric(1)) Number observations retrieve end series. NULL, restriction applied. Default NULL. updated_after (NULL | character(1)) ISO 8601 timestamp retrieve observations updated given time (e.g., "2024-06-01T00:00:00"). NULL, restriction applied. Default NULL.

title: Fetch Norges Bank (NoB) data — nob_data
text: flow (character(1)) dataflow query. See nob_metadata() available dataflows. key (NULL | character(1)) series key query using dot-separated dimension values (e.g., "B.USD.NOK.SP"). Use + multiple values one dimension (e.g., "B.USD+EUR.NOK.SP"). NULL, data flow returned. Default NULL. start_period (NULL | character(1) | integer(1)) Start date data (e.g., "2024-01-01" 2024). NULL, start date restriction applied. Default NULL. end_period (NULL | character(1) | integer(1)) End date data, format start_period. NULL, end date restriction applied. Default NULL. first_n (NULL | numeric(1)) Number observations retrieve start series. NULL, restriction applied. Default NULL. last_n (NULL | numeric(1)) Number observations retrieve end series. NULL, restriction applied. Default NULL.

                                                The policy rate is the interest rate at which the banks can borrow or deposit in the Riksbank over a 7-day period. The policy rate is the Riksbank's most important key rate.\nThe policy rate was called The repo rate until June 8, 2022.  #>   2: The deposit rate is the rate of interest banks receive when they deposit funds in their accounts at the Riksbank overnight and is always 0.75 percentage points lower than the policy rate. The deposit rate is the floor of what is known as the interest rate corridor and is one of the Riksbank's three key rates. #>   3:                           The lending rate is the rate of interest banks pay when they borrow overnight funds from the Riksbank and is always 0.75 percentage points higher than the policy rate. The lending rate is known as the ceiling of the interest rate corridor and is one of the Riksbank's three key rates. #>   4:                                                                                     The liquidity facility rate is the interest rate for the lending that the Riksbank offers to the banks in the supplementary liquidity facility. This interest rate is currently 0.75 percentage points higher than the policy rate #>   5:                                                                                         The marginal rate was the Riksbank's key rate from December 1985 until the end of May 1994. title: bbk (development version)
text: Support Banco de Portugal (BdP) data. Support Bank Japan (BoJ) data.

title: bbk 0.9.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-21 Add optional caching API responses via options(bbk.cache = TRUE). Cached responses stored 1 day default can customized options(bbk.cache_max_age = seconds). Use bbk_cache_dir() find cache location bbk_cache_clear() clear . Support Bank International Settlements (BIS) data. Support Norges Bank (NoB) data. boc_data() now returns value column instead rate consistency data functions. bde_latest() returns recently published value one BdE series. bbk_dimension() returns dimension structure given Bundesbank dataflow. bis_dimension() returns dimension structure given BIS dataflow. ecb_data() gains updated_after parameter retrieve observations updated given timestamp. ecb_dimension() returns dimension structure given ECB dataflow. nob_dimension() returns dimension structure given Norges Bank dataflow. snb_dimension() returns dimension structure given SNB cube. srb_calendar() returns Swedish banking calendar day information. srb_cross_rates() computes cross exchange rates two Riksbank currency series. Support Sveriges Riksbank (SRb) data.

title: bbk 0.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-10-01 Add missing data bbk_series(). Support exchange rates Bank Canada (BoC). Support Bank Canada (BoC) data.
